refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize safety without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. No multiple obligations are present that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria defined by stakeholders, such as latency p95 ≤ 150 ms.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Use a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service for teleoperation data
Incorporate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms to maintain latency within limits
Segment teleoperation functions into modular components to isolate latency-sensitive operations
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules could increase coupling
Shared resources causing contention and delays
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on architectural design to minimize latency and supports modular separation of concerns, which helps maintain independence between components.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Latency measurement and control mechanisms are accurate and responsive
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency management as a DP to handle variable network conditions and ensure consistent teleoperation safety.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flows.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data stream for real-time telemetry data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface for data transmission and reception.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is the core system handling real-time processing; minimal diffusion as most processing is internal.
Vehicle module handles physical operations; real-time data processing is only partially contained, leading to significant diffusion.
Teleoperation involves remote control and may require some real-time data, but not the full architecture, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not core real-time data processing, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP as it is designed to handle core system architecture and data processing, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation for some real-time data handling may cause interface complexity.
Integration with Vehicle module may require careful interface management to avoid data buffering delays.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment issues persist, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing layers, allowing clearer module boundaries and reducing diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving the required teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module for some real-time data handling may cause interface complexity.
Integration with Vehicle module may require careful interface management to avoid data buffering delays.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating good containment of the DP. However, moderate to high diffusion in related modules (Teleoperation, Vehicle, Safety Services) suggests some risk of scattered responsibilities that need interface management.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring end-to-end latency metrics via the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces supporting the required data flows for teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing layers to clarify module boundaries and reduce diffusion.
Enhance interface definitions between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation/Vehicle modules to minimize buffering delays and interface complexity.
Monitor and manage diffusion risks by consolidating real-time data processing responsibilities within the NGHE Solution module where possible.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed indicating that the current DP and IM adequately address the FR, the presence of diffusion risks and interface complexity noted in the dp_im_gate suggests that the current decomposition can be improved. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to refine the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing layers to clarify module boundaries and reduce diffusion. Enhancing interface definitions and consolidating real-time data processing responsibilities will reduce risks and improve architectural clarity before proceeding to further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly separate data acquisition and processing layers, clarifying module boundaries and reducing diffusion risks, thereby improving containment and interface management in a safety-critical system.

assumptions: The current DP logically addresses the FR with real-time data processing and minimal buffering.
The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and low diffusion, but related modules show moderate to high diffusion.
Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible and allocation is clear.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity due to overlap with Teleoperation module.
Risk of buffering delays from integration with Vehicle module.
Scattered responsibilities causing diffusion across modules.
evidence_needed: Refined DP specification separating data acquisition and processing layers.
Updated interface definitions between NGHE Solution and related modules.
Verification results monitoring end-to-end latency metrics after refinement.
